title: Evaluating the impact of the Hepatitis B Foundation's international storytelling program on empowerment, advocacy, and stigma reduction.
abstract: Hepatitis B remains a major global public health challenge, characterized by low awareness, underdiagnosis, limited access to care, and persistent stigma. Storytelling has emerged as a promising health communication strategy to humanize disease experiences, reduce stigma, and promote advocacy. This study evaluates the Hepatitis B Foundation's International Storytelling Program and its impact on storytellers' empowerment, confidence, stigma experiences, and advocacy engagement.
